name: Stream Power Erosion Model Behavioral Economics Dynamics
mechanism: Stream Power Erosion Model in geomorphology operates through the interaction of channel reach as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through discharge, channel slope, and substrate erodibility, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—erosion rate proportional to unit stream power exceeding threshold—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by channel incision and landscape denudation rate. This process is bounded by bedrock vs alluvial channel, discharge variability, tectonic uplift, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to behavioral economics, the mechanism from geomorphology suggests that the core units of behavioral economics undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (discharge, channel slope, and substrate erodibility) maps onto the generative processes in behavioral economics, while the selection pressure (erosion rate proportional to unit stream power exceeding threshold) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that behavioral economics systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in geomorphology, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (bedrock vs alluvial channel, discharge variability, tectonic uplift) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in behavioral economics designed using geomorphology structural principles will show 20% higher performance scores compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=40 cases over a 6-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Coral Holobiont Feedback Theory Sandcastle Engineering Algebra
mechanism: Coral Holobiont Feedback Theory in marine biology operates through the interaction of coral holobiont (coral host plus symbiotic microbiome) as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through stochastic acquisition and shuffling of Symbiodiniaceae clades and bacterial consortia, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—thermal stress events differentially eliminate holobionts with maladapted symbiont communities—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by maximized photosynthetic yield and calcification rate under variable thermal regimes. This process is bounded by breaks down when bleaching events exceed the adaptive capacity of any available symbiont combination, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to sandcastle engineering, the mechanism from marine biology suggests that the core units of sandcastle engineering undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (stochastic acquisition and shuffling of Symbiodiniaceae clades and bacterial consortia) maps onto the generative processes in sandcastle engineering, while the selection pressure (thermal stress events differentially eliminate holobionts with maladapted symbiont communities) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that sandcastle engineering systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in marine biology, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (breaks down when bleaching events exceed the adaptive capacity of any available symbiont combination) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in sandcastle engineering designed using marine biology structural principles will show 22% lower error rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=200 cases over a 18-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Gresham's Law Debasement Spiral Beekeeping / Apiculture Principle
mechanism: Gresham's Law Debasement Spiral in numismatics operates through the interaction of circulating coin as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through mints issue coins with varying metal purity under fiscal pressure, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—holders hoard high-purity coins and spend debased ones—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by circulation velocity of the debased coin; hoarding rate of the pure coin. This process is bounded by legal tender laws, detection difficulty, public trust threshold, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

name: SLAM Machine Learning Paradigm
mechanism: SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) in robotics operates through the interaction of robot pose + map landmark as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through sensor observations with noise, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—probabilistic filter (EKF, particle filter) updates joint pose-map belief—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by consistent map and accurate localization. This process is bounded by sensor range and noise, loop-closure detection, dynamic environments, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to machine learning, the mechanism from robotics suggests that the core units of machine learning undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The boundary conditions (sensor range and noise, loop-closure detection, dynamic environments) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in machine learning designed using robotics structural principles will show 25% reduction in failure rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=40 cases over a 24-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Red Queen Hypothesis Neurolinguistics Model
mechanism: Red Queen Hypothesis in parasitology operates through the interaction of host-parasite genotype pair as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through host and parasite genotype frequencies, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—co-evolutionary arms race: rare host genotypes favored, parasite tracks common ones—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by infection resistance (host) / infectivity (parasite). This process is bounded by sexually reproducing host, obligate parasite, overlapping generations, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

prediction: Systems in neurolinguistics designed using parasitology structural principles will show 22% lower error rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=80 cases over a 24-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Mercator Conformal-Area Tradeoff Category Theory Topology
mechanism: Mercator Conformal-Area Tradeoff in map projection operates through the interaction of infinitesimal circle (Tissot indicatrix) at a given latitude as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through projection equations stretch the indicatrix differently in area, shape, and direction at each point, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—navigational use selects conformal projections (angle-preserving); thematic mapping selects equal-area—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by minimization of the distortion metric relevant to the map's intended use (angular error or area ratio). This process is bounded by no projection can preserve both area and angles simultaneously (Gauss's Theorema Egregium consequence), establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to category theory, the mechanism from map projection suggests that the core units of category theory undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (projection equations stretch the indicatrix differently in area, shape, and direction at each point) maps onto the generative processes in category theory, while the selection pressure (navigational use selects conformal projections (angle-preserving); thematic mapping selects equal-area) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that category theory systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in map projection, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (no projection can preserve both area and angles simultaneously (Gauss's Theorema Egregium consequence)) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in category theory designed using map projection structural principles will show 15% improvement in efficiency metrics compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=100 cases over a 8-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Branch Position Training via Wiring Ecology Protocol
mechanism: Branch Position Training via Wiring in bonsai cultivation operates through the interaction of branch segment under wire constraint as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through wire gauge, wrapping angle, and application timing interact with branch lignification rate and growth direction, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—branches wired during active growth set into position as wood lignifies; those wired too late or too loosely spring back—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by permanent angular placement matching the target design silhouette. This process is bounded by branch diameter-to-wire gauge ratio, lignification speed, wire-scar risk window, seasonal growth phase, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to ecology, the mechanism from bonsai cultivation suggests that the core units of ecology undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The boundary conditions (branch diameter-to-wire gauge ratio, lignification speed, wire-scar risk window, seasonal growth phase) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in ecology designed using bonsai cultivation structural principles will show 25% reduction in failure rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=40 cases over a 24-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.
